description: Contrasts 401 (token itself does not resolve to a device) against 403 (token resolves fine; resolved role is not permitted on this route). Matches v1-architecture.md §3 and openapi.yaml #/components/responses/Unauthorized, #/components/responses/Forbidden. Uses POST /v1/automations, which is owner only (excludes both viewer and source).

case_401_revoked_token.note: Token hash no longer resolves to a live device row — this device was revoked via DELETE /v1/devices/:id, and revocation is effective on the very next request (v1-architecture.md §10.2). The credential is well-formed (matches the sr1 grammar) but its device record is gone.

case_403_wrong_role.note: Token resolves fine — this is the viewer device from join.json (dev_9c4e2b7a1f, role viewer). POST /v1/automations is owner only (v1-architecture.md §3); PATCH/DELETE would have succeeded for this same viewer token.
